text:
There once was a lonely cat who lived in a sunlit room. The cat had no friend except for the gentle golden hours that would stretch out of the window each day. One bright afternoon, as the sun climbed high into the sky, a curious thing happened.

From outside the window, a bird fluttered into view. At first, the cat shunned this newfound visitor. But the bird chirped sweetly, and the cat felt compelled to observe. It perched on a window perch, singing a merry tune.

The cat pounced onto the window seat and found itself admiring the songbird not for fear but for an unexpected kinship. Their eyes locked, and instead of feeling alone, the cat felt understood.

The bird tilted its head amusingly as if to say, "What fun! You intrigued me." And so the friendship blossomed. Every day the bird would visit, each time introducing a new song. The cat listened intently as it never had before.

Slowly
